text:
I'll search for this reference to verify the details.
Based on my web search, I found the exact article at the cited URL. The search results confirm that the article "How Objective is Peer Review?" was published on Communications of the ACM (CACM) blog on November 18, 2020, and authored by Hannah Bast.

The automated checker's metadata mismatch (showing "Cacm" as the actual author instead of "Hannah Bast") is a classification error on the checker's part—it incorrectly identified the publication venue/organization as the author field. The actual content page clearly shows Hannah Bast as the author and Communications of the ACM as the venue.

EXPLANATION: The exact article was found on the cited URL with Hannah Bast correctly identified as the author and published on Communications of the ACM blog in November 2020. The automated checker's author mismatch is a metadata extraction error, not evidence of hallucination.

LINK: https://cacm.acm.org/blogcacm/how-objective-is-peer-review/

FOUND_TITLE: How Objective is Peer Review?

FOUND_AUTHORS: Hannah Bast

FOUND_YEAR: 2020

VERDICT: UNLIKELY
